folio.label: 66v
folio.l: That whilom , ©e han sent , in to Surrye
It am I fader , that in the salt See
Was put allone , and dampned for to deye
Now good fader , mercy I ©ow crye
Send me nomore , vn to noon hethenes
But thanketú my lord , heere , of his kyndnes
¢ Who can the pytous , ioye , tellen alle
Be twixe hem three , syn they be thus I mette
But of my tale , make an ende I shalle
The day gootú fast , I wyl no lengere lette
This glad folk , to dyner they hem sette
In ioye & blisse , at mete I lete he~ dwelle
A , M&lbar; , fold , moore than I can telle
¢ This child Maurice , was sithen Em¹our
Mad by the Pope , and lyued cristenly
To cristes chirche , he dide gret honour
But I lete al , this story passen by
Of Custaunce is my tale , specialy
In the olde Romayne gestes , mounö men fynde
Maurices lyf , I bere it nat in mynde
¢ The kyngö Alla , whan he his tyme sey
Witú his Custaunce , his holy wyf so swete
To Englond ben they come , the rigút wey
Wheere as they lyue , in ioye and in quiete
But litel while it lastetú , I ©ow hete
Ioye of this world , for tyme wol nat a byde
Fro day to nygút , it chaungetú as the tyde
¢ Who leued euäe , in swicú delyt , oo , day
That hi~ ne meeued , other conscience
Or ire , or talent , or som maner affray
Envye , or pryde , or passiou~ , or offence
I ne seye , but for this ende , this sentence
That litel while , in ioye , or in plesaunce
Lastetú the blisse , xxofxxof Alla , ofw of w Custaunceof1043It is written on an erasure.ODR
¢ For detú that taketú , of hey & lowe his rente
Whan passed was a ©ere , euene as I gesse
Out of this world , this kyngö Alla he hente
For whom Custaunce , hatú ful gret heuynesse
Now lat vs preye to god , his soule blisse
And Dame Custaunce , fynaly to seye
Toward the toun of Rome , gootú hire weye
¢ To Rome is come , this holy creature
And fyndetú hire frendes , hole and sounde
Now is she skaped , al hire auenture
